audit_reverification: 2026-05-30: Fixed OpenTelemetry v1/v2 dependency misalignment in packages/observability (sdk-trace-node, sdk-metrics, resources upgraded to ^2.7.0; exporters and instrumentation-http to ^0.218.0; Resource class replaced with resourceFromAttributes() factory). Removed inapplicable .github/workflows/brakeman.yml (Ruby scanner on TS project). All gates pass cold: typecheck 145/145, test 146/146, build 115/115.

gate_notes.typecheck: 145/145 on 2026-05-30 after aligning @opentelemetry packages to v2 generation (resources ^2.7.0, sdk-trace-node ^2.7.0, sdk-metrics ^2.7.0, exporter-trace-otlp-http ^0.218.0, exporter-metrics-otlp-http ^0.218.0, instrumentation-http ^0.218.0). Source updated to use resourceFromAttributes() instead of deprecated new Resource() constructor.
gate_notes.build: 115/115 on 2026-05-30. All packages build cleanly with --concurrency=3.
gate_notes.test: 146/146 on 2026-05-30. All test suites pass. NOTE: the separate coverage job (vitest run --coverage) is RED due to real product line coverage ~30% < 50% threshold (genuine test debt, BUG-2). Not faked.
gate_notes.brakeman_removed: Removed .github/workflows/brakeman.yml - Ruby security scanner (Brakeman) was running on a pure TypeScript/Node.js monorepo with no Ruby code. It generated false positives and added CI noise.

critical_risks: Coverage debt (BUG-2): real product line coverage ~30% vs 50% global threshold - test-and-coverage CI job is red until tests are written.
ci(22) parallel-Next-build OOM mitigation (PR #93) not yet confirmed green on the GitHub runner.
